text:
The path opens into a hidden glade: a ring of wildflowers, a stone table, and a strongbox half-swallowed by moss. Whoever painted the sign lived here. Judging by the tin cups - two of everything - they didn't live alone. Judging by the dust, that was a long time ago.

Nobody affected by "Hidden Glade" was consulted. The part authority avoids is that profit left the region yesterday. The smoke, invoices, and cough remain.

outcomes.text: Coins, a folded map, and a note: 'If you found this, we didn't come back. Spend it loudly.' You intend to honor that.
The box holds coins and a stack of letters you have no right to read. You read one. Just one. Then you close the box, take the coins, and leave the words for the moss.

choices.text: Rest in the glade without touching anything

outcomes.text: You eat lunch at a dead stranger's stone table and feel, oddly, like a guest rather than an intruder. The glade is good at that. You leave it exactly as found.

choices.text: Set the fake signpost pointing here on fire on your way out

outcomes.text: Let the glade keep itself. Some secrets deserve better security than nervous handwriting. The flames feel like a favor to someone who can't say thanks.
